final.summary:
Tailoring log for: Civil Engineer.

- Profile summary: revised (heuristic: generic-leaning phrasing present: cross-functional).
- sectionVisibility: structure or presentation updated.
- Experience id 16: small text tweak (minor).

tailorExplanation.paragraph: I treated this as a stretch match for Civil Engineer at Army Civilian Careers, so I focused on transferable proof instead of pretending every JD term was already in your background. The strongest direct overlap I found was engineer; the rest of the draft bridges from nearby evidence where your resume supports it. Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story. The biggest help here: reframed your experience around the job's strongest signals, gave the summary a clearer role-specific angle, and adjusted the layout so the strongest sections show up sooner. Under the hood, I changed the summary, experience, and which sections show based on those signals.

details.title: What I prioritized
items: Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story.
I prioritized direct evidence first, transferable proof, measurable outcomes, and claim caution.

details.title: Why it changed
items: Target story: Quinn Taylor has a background in software engineering with transferable skills in project management, automation, and technical collaboration, suitab...
Main evidence themes: workflow / automation and coordination / communication.

details.title: Fit risk
items: The posting has many unsupported role terms, so the draft should stay conservative and visibly transferable.
Bridge only from evidenced work and keep unsupported expectations in review language.

details.title: Fit read
items: Stretch fit: be transparent and conservative. Frame transferable strengths, keep useful non-keyword evidence, and explicitly avoid unsupported target-role claims.
Transferable evidence I considered: BitGo Software Engineering Intern, Taylor.io – AI Resume Builder & Workflow Platform, and Centi – Personal Finance App.

details.title: Not directly evidenced
items: I did not claim civil engineering structures, corrective action, criteria or specifications, design and construction, project design documents, construction, submittals, and corrective because I could not verify that exact experience in the resume.

details.title: Before you send
items: Do not treat weak keyword overlap as direct experience.
I treated civil engineering structures, correcti as review items, not resume claims, because I could not verify them in the resume.
I did not claim heat transfer, abet, and transcripts because that exact evidence was not in the resume.
Some JD terms were not directly evidenced: civil engineering structures, corrective action, criteria or specifications, and design and construction.
A row may still be close to the original even after repair, so it is worth a quick read.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story.
claimRules: Do not treat weak keyword overlap as direct experience.

education.school: University of Central Florida (GPA 3.78 / 4.0) August 2021 – May 2025

experience.title: Software Engineering Intern
experience.description:
• Developed centralized scheduling and monitoring tools that reduced manual task management, debugging, and log-checking time by ~3 hours per week for internal IT teams.
• Built internal automation tools that allowed IT and compliance teams to schedule, monitor, and manage operational workflows through a centralized web platform.
• Developed task orchestration and backend processing workflows using Django, Celery, Redis, and PostgreSQL to support scheduled jobs, logging, and live task updates.
• Worked closely with design teams, engineers, and offshore developers to build and refine frontend workflows, backend APIs, and real-time operational tooling.
experience.skills: Python, Django, React, PostgreSQL, AWS EC2

projects.title: Taylor.io – AI Resume Builder & Workflow Platform
projects.description:
• Built an AI-powered resume platform that stores structured user career data and generates tailored resumes based on specific job descriptions.
• Developed workflows that extract relevant keywords and requirements from job postings, then use OpenAI APIs to generate targeted resume content from user profile data.
• Built FastAPI backend services for resume parsing, authentication, and real-time editing using PostgreSQL and React.
• Used AI-assisted coding workflows with tools like Cursor to rapidly prototype, iterate, and refine product features while maintaining production-style architecture.

projects.title: Centi – Personal Finance App
projects.description:
• Built a personal finance platform that aggregates bank accounts, transactions, and spending activity into one clear dashboard, helping users understand their finances without manually sorting through multiple apps and statements..
• Developed transaction processing and categorization workflows using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and Plaid APIs to organize messy financial data into searchable spending insights and summaries.
• Created customizable React dashboards that let users explore spending trends, account activity, and financial patterns in the way that makes the most sense to them.

projects.title: SentimentTrader – Financial Sentiment & Market Analysis Pipeline
projects.description:
• Built an end-to-end analytics pipeline processing 1K+ finance-related Reddit posts per run through automated ETL workflows, combining market data and sentiment analysis to generate trading signals for users.
• Developed preprocessing and feature engineering workflows that transform unstructured discussions, engagement metrics, and stock data into structured ticker-level indicators for analysis.
• Trained and evaluated XGBoost models achieving 55–70% ROC-AUC while comparing sentiment patterns against short-term market movement.

projects.title: ShelfVision – Dense Retail Shelf Object Detector
projects.description:
• Built a retail analysis system trained on 11K+ images to detect and identify products in cluttered shelf environments.
• Developed preprocessing and training workflows that organize image data, generate training inputs, and improve detection performance across different shelf layouts.

summary.summary: Quinn Taylor is a software engineer with a strong foundation in automation and project management, demonstrating transferable skills relevant to civil engineering roles. Experienced in developing workflows and tools that enhance operational efficiency, Quinn is adept at collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver impactful solutions.
